description: Buffer flush precedence: when an event both reaches `limit` and matches `until`, `limit` takes precedence (it is evaluated first), so the event is flushed as part of the batch rather than treated as an excluded delimiter.

fixtures.name: Buffer limit precedence over until
fixtures.description: src.emit produces two events from one input; the second matches the buffer's `until` schema AND reaches `limit: 2`. Because limit is evaluated first, the batch flushes including the second event ([{n:1},{n:2,end:true}]) rather than excluding it ([{n:1}]).

graph.description: Buffer with both limit and until; the limit-reaching event also matches until.
